secondary_meaning: to touch one's heart or feelings
usage_notes: Often used in passive: être ému(e). Can mean to affect deeply.
common_collocations: être ému, émouvoir quelqu'un, profondément ému
image_search_query: person touched emotionally or moved to tears
image_context: A person with tears in eyes watching a touching movie
example_1: Ce film m'a beaucoup ému.
example_2: Sa mort m'a ému aux larmes.
example_2_translation: His death moved me to tears.
example_3: L'histoire l'a profondément émue.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. -ouvoir ending rhymes with pouvoir.
memory_hook: Émouvoir - sounds like 'émotion' - to cause emotion
common_mistakes: Don't confuse with 'mouvoir' (to move physically)

primary_meaning: disorientation, feeling of being in a foreign place
secondary_meaning: the state of being out of one's element
usage_notes: Positive connotation often (excitement of new places); negative (discomfort abroad)
common_collocations: le dépaysement total, sentiment de dépaysement, éprouver un dépaysement
image_search_query: traveler looking confused at foreign city map or lost signs
image_context: A traveler looking confused at a foreign city map
example_1: Le dépaysement est total quand on voyage.
example_1_translation: The disorientation is total when traveling.
example_2: J'aime le dépaysement des grandes villes.
example_2_translation: I like the disorientation of big cities.
example_3: Ce dépaysement m'a d'abord troublé.
example_3_translation: This feeling of being out of place initially troubled me.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on third syllable (pay). -ement is silent 'm'.
memory_hook: Dépaysement - 'pays' (country) - not in your country
common_mistakes: It's a noun, not an adjective; don't use as 'dépaysé'

secondary_meaning: a sentimental longing for the past
usage_notes: Often used with 'de' (nostalgia for) or 'pour' (nostalgia for)
common_collocations: avoir de la nostalgie, nostalgie pour/de, vague de nostalgie
image_search_query: old photo album with vintage sepia photographs or memories
image_context: An old photo album with vintage sepia photographs
example_1: J'ai de la nostalgie pour mon enfance.
example_1_translation: I feel nostalgia for my childhood.
example_2: La nostalgie m'envahit parfois.
example_2_translation: Nostalgia sometimes overwhelms me.
example_3: Une vague de nostalgie l'a saisie.
example_3_translation: A wave of nostalgia seized her.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. -algie combines Greek nostos (home) + algos (pain)
memory_hook: Nostalgie - nostos (home) + algie (pain) - homesickness
common_mistakes: Remember: nostalgia can be joyful, not always sad

usage_notes: Agrees with noun gender/number. Often with 'de' or 'pour'
common_collocations: inquiet de, inquiet pour, être inquiet, air inquiet
image_search_query: person with worried expression or anxious gestures
image_context: A person with worried expression holding their head
example_1: Je suis inquiet pour sa santé.
example_2: Ne sois pas inquiet, tout ira bien.
example_2_translation: Don't worry, everything will be fine.
example_3: Elle avait un regard inquiet.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Feminine: inquiète; plural: inquiets/inquiètes
memory_hook: Inquiet - in-quiet - not quiet/peaceful
common_mistakes: Feminine changes to inquiète (adds grave accent)

secondary_meaning: to ease one's burden or pain
usage_notes: Takes direct object. Can be reflexive: se soulager
common_collocations: soulager la douleur, soulager quelqu'un, être soulagé
image_search_query: person in relief after stress melting away or comfort
image_context: A person in relief sighing after stress melts away
example_1: Ce médicament va soulager ta douleur.
example_1_translation: This medicine will relieve your pain.
example_2: Il m'a soulagé de mes inquiétudes.
example_2_translation: He freed me from my worries.
example_3: Je suis soulagé que tout soit résolu.
example_3_translation: I'm relieved that everything is resolved.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. -ger adds 'e' in conjugation (soulageons)
memory_hook: Soulager - sous (under) + alléger (lighten) - lighten the load
common_mistakes: Don't confuse with 'alléger' (to lighten); soulager is for pain/worry

secondary_meaning: the limit of one's knowledge or interests
usage_notes: Literal (line where sky/water meet) or figurative (scope of knowledge)
common_collocations: à l'horizon, nouvel horizon, horizon bouché
image_search_query: sunset horizon over ocean or landscape vista
image_context: A vast sunset horizon over calm ocean waters
example_1: Le soleil se couche à l'horizon.
example_1_translation: The sun sets on the horizon.
example_2: De nouveaux horizons s'ouvrent à moi.
example_2_translation: New horizons are opening up for me.
example_3: À l'horizon, on voyait les montagnes.
example_3_translation: On the horizon, one could see the mountains.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. First vowel is short 'o'
memory_hook: Horizon - horizon in English, from Greek horos (boundary)
common_mistakes: It's masculine: l'horizon (not la)

usage_notes: Feminine noun. Often used: faire sa valise (pack)
common_collocations: faire une valise, défaire sa valise, valise à roulettes
image_search_query: suitcase open with clothes packed or luggage
image_context: A red suitcase open on a bed with clothes packed
example_1: J'ai fait ma valise pour le voyage.
example_1_translation: I packed my suitcase for the trip.
example_2: La valise est trop lourde.
example_2_translation: The suitcase is too heavy.
example_3: Elle a déballé sa valise à l'hôtel.
example_3_translation: She unpacked her suitcase at the hotel.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable. Feminine article: la
memory_hook: Valise - looks like English 'valid' but means suitcase in FR
common_mistakes: It's feminine: la valise, not le valise

secondary_meaning: the moment or place of arriving
usage_notes: Feminine. Often paired with 'départ' (departure)
common_collocations: à l'arrivée, à mon arrivée, heure d'arrivée
image_search_query: plane landing or person arriving at destination
image_context: A plane landing on a runway at sunset
example_1: L'arrivée à l'aéroport est prévue à 15h.
example_1_translation: Arrival at the airport is scheduled for 3pm.
example_2: Nous attendons son arrivée.
example_2_translation: We're waiting for his arrival.
example_3: À son arrivée, on l'a applaudie.
example_3_translation: Upon her arrival, they applauded her.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable. Double r sound.
memory_hook: Arrivée - arrive + ée (feminine) - to arrive
common_mistakes: It's feminine: l'arrivée; derived from verb arriver

secondary_meaning: the beginning of a journey or process
usage_notes: Masculine. Common in schedules: heure de départ
common_collocations: heure de départ, à mon départ, départ immédiat
image_search_query: train departing or person leaving on journey
image_context: A train departing from a French railway station
example_1: Le départ du train est à 8h.
example_2: Il prend son départ demain matin.
example_2_translation: He's leaving tomorrow morning.
example_3: Au départ, j'était nerveux.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Masculine: le départ
memory_hook: Départ - de (from) + part (part/direction) - leaving from a place
common_mistakes: It's masculine: le départ, not la départ

secondary_meaning: the place where one is accommodated
usage_notes: Can be abstract (the service) or concrete (the place itself)
common_collocations: hébergement gratuit, hébergement hôtel, trouver un hébergement
image_search_query: hotel room or cozy lodging accommodation
image_context: A cozy hotel room with windows overlooking a city
example_1: Nous avons trouvé un hébergement rapide.
example_2: L'hébergement coûte cher en ville.
example_2_translation: Accommodation is expensive in the city.
example_3: L'hébergement et le repas étaient inclus.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. Silent 't' at end: hébergement
memory_hook: Hébergement - héberger (to host) + ment (noun suffix)
common_mistakes: It's masculine: l'hébergement, with silent final 't'

secondary_meaning: a short journey for leisure or education
usage_notes: Often organized tours; implies return to base
common_collocations: faire une excursion, excursion guidée, excursion en montagne
image_search_query: hikers or group on outdoor adventure trip
image_context: A group hiking in mountains with backpacks
example_1: Nous faisons une excursion en montagne.
example_2: L'excursion dure toute la journée.
example_2_translation: The excursion lasts all day.
example_3: L'excursion était bien organisée.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. French pronunciation with hard 'k' sound
memory_hook: Excursion - ex (out) + cursio (running) - going out on a run
common_mistakes: Feminine: l'excursion (not le); not 'excurision'

usage_notes: Often used as: passer la douane (go through customs)
common_collocations: à la douane, passer la douane, douanier
image_search_query: customs booth with officer checking luggage
image_context: Airport customs booth with officer checking luggage
example_1: Je dois passer la douane.
example_1_translation: I have to go through customs.
example_2: Les objets à déclarer à la douane.
example_2_translation: Items to declare at customs.
example_3: À la douane, on m'a posé beaucoup de questions.
example_3_translation: At customs, they asked me many questions.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Feminine: la douane
memory_hook: Douane - from Arabic 'diwan' - tax office/customs
common_mistakes: It's feminine: la douane, not le douane

usage_notes: Masculine. Found in stations, airports, banks
common_collocations: au guichet, guichet de vente, guichet automatique
image_search_query: ticket counter or service window with clerk
image_context: A railway ticket counter with a clerk serving customer
example_1: J'ai attendu au guichet pendant une heure.
example_1_translation: I waited at the ticket window for an hour.
example_2: Le guichet est fermé le dimanche.
example_2_translation: The counter is closed on Sundays.
example_3: Il y a une longue file au guichet.
example_3_translation: There's a long line at the counter.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Silent 'et' ending: guichet
memory_hook: Guichet - from Italian 'sportello' (small door/window)
common_mistakes: It's masculine: le guichet, not la guichet

usage_notes: Usually uncountable; rarely pluralized
common_collocations: bon santé, mauvaise santé, problème de santé
image_search_query: healthy lifestyle with fruits vegetables or exercise
image_context: Fresh vegetables and fruits representing healthy eating
example_1: La santé est précieuse.
example_2: Il a des problèmes de santé.
example_2_translation: He has health problems.
example_3: À votre santé! (toast)
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable. -é silent at end
memory_hook: Santé - sans (without) + té (pain) - without pain = health
common_mistakes: It's feminine and usually uncountable: la santé

secondary_meaning: a sign or indication of disease
usage_notes: Can be figurative: signs of something
common_collocations: symptôme de, symptôme inquiétant, premiers symptômes
image_search_query: doctor consultation with patient describing health symptoms
image_context: A doctor consultation with patient describing symptoms
example_1: Quels sont vos symptômes?
example_2: Le symptôme persiste depuis trois jours.
example_2_translation: The symptom has persisted for three days.
example_3: Les premiers symptômes sont apparus hier.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. French: ô (circumflex accent)
memory_hook: Symptôme - syn (together) + ptôme (fall) - symptoms fall together
common_mistakes: It's masculine: le symptôme; ô accent is important

secondary_meaning: a doctor's written instruction for medicine
usage_notes: Feminine. Also means 'arrangement' in archaic French
common_collocations: selon l'ordonnance, remplir une ordonnance, ordonnance médicale
image_search_query: doctor prescription paper with medical handwriting
image_context: A doctor's prescription paper with medical handwriting
example_1: Le médecin m'a donné une ordonnance.
example_1_translation: The doctor gave me a prescription.
example_2: Je vais chercher mes médicaments à la pharmacie avec l'ordonnance.
example_2_translation: I'm going to get my medicine at the pharmacy with the prescription.
example_3: L'ordonnance est valable un mois.
example_3_translation: The prescription is valid for one month.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. Feminine: l'ordonnance
memory_hook: Ordonnance - ordonner (to order/prescribe) + ance
common_mistakes: It's feminine: l'ordonnance, not le; not 'ordonnation'

usage_notes: Masculine. 'Attraper un rhume' = to catch a cold
common_collocations: attraper un rhume, rhume des foins, guérir un rhume
image_search_query: sick person with tissues or runny nose
image_context: A person with tissue box and runny nose looking sick
example_1: J'ai attrapé un rhume cet hiver.
example_1_translation: I caught a cold this winter.
example_2: Un rhume dure généralement une semaine.
example_2_translation: A cold usually lasts a week.
example_3: J'ai un rhume de cerveau.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Silent 'h' in beginning
memory_hook: Rhume - from Latin 'rheuma' (flow/discharge)
common_mistakes: It's masculine: le rhume, not la; silent 'h'

usage_notes: Feminine. Often used: avoir une allergie à
common_collocations: allergie à, allergie alimentaire, crise d'allergie
image_search_query: allergic reaction on skin or pollen allergy symptoms
image_context: Red allergic skin rash with pollen particles on hand
example_1: J'ai une allergie aux pollens.
example_2: Il doit éviter les aliments à cause de son allergie.
example_2_translation: He must avoid foods because of his allergy.
example_3: L'allergie s'est manifestée soudainement.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. Feminine: l'allergie
memory_hook: Allergie - allo (other) + ergie (work) - reaction to foreign substance
common_mistakes: It's feminine: l'allergie, not le; pronounced aller-zhee

usage_notes: Feminine. 'Avoir de la fièvre' = to have a fever
common_collocations: avoir de la fièvre, fièvre jaune, montée de fièvre
image_search_query: thermometer showing high temperature or fever
image_context: A thermometer showing high temperature on a red scale
example_1: Elle a de la fièvre depuis hier.
example_1_translation: She has had a fever since yesterday.
example_2: Le médecin dit que la fièvre va baisser.
example_2_translation: The doctor says the fever will go down.
example_3: La fièvre l'épuisait.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Feminine: la fièvre
memory_hook: Fièvre - from Latin 'febris' - fever/heat
common_mistakes: It's feminine: la fièvre, not le; pronounced fee-èvr

usage_notes: Feminine. Often with 'avoir une douleur à'
common_collocations: douleur à, sourire malgré la douleur, soulager la douleur
image_search_query: person holding painful body part or in discomfort
image_context: A person holding their painful shoulder in discomfort
example_1: J'ai une douleur à l'épaule.
example_1_translation: I have a pain in my shoulder.
example_2: La douleur est supportable.
example_2_translation: The pain is bearable.
example_3: La douleur a disparu après le traitement.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable. Feminine: la douleur
memory_hook: Douleur - doux (sweet) + eur (negative) - opposite of sweet
common_mistakes: It's feminine: la douleur, not le; pronounced doo-leur

usage_notes: Masculine. Common word for any shop
common_collocations: au magasin, grand magasin, magasin de vetements
image_search_query: colorful storefront with items displayed in windows
image_context: A colorful storefront with items displayed in windows
example_1: Je vais au magasin achèter du pain.
example_1_translation: I'm going to the store to buy bread.
example_2: Le magasin est ouvert jusqu'à 20h.
example_2_translation: The store is open until 8pm.
example_3: Il travaille dans un grand magasin.
example_3_translation: He works in a large department store.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. Masculine: le magasin
memory_hook: Magasin - from Arabic 'mahzan' (warehouse)
common_mistakes: It's masculine: le magasin, not la; pronounced mag-a-zin

secondary_meaning: period of reduced prices on goods
usage_notes: Usually plural: les soldes. Specific seasonal event in France
common_collocations: en soldes, période de soldes, prix des soldes
image_search_query: store with SALE signs and crowded shoppers
image_context: A store with giant SALE signs and crowded shoppers
example_1: Les soldes commencent en janvier.
example_2: Il y a de bonnes affaires pendant les soldes.
example_2_translation: There are good deals during the sales.
example_3: J'ai acheté cet habit en soldes.
example_3_translation: I bought this outfit on sale.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Usually plural: les soldes
memory_hook: Soldes - from Latin 'solidus' (solid payment) - settling accounts
common_mistakes: Usually plural: les soldes, not le solde (which is accounting balance)

secondary_meaning: shelf or section in a store
usage_notes: Masculine. Common in supermarkets and stores
common_collocations: rayon de vêtements, rayon des produits frais, responsable du rayon
image_search_query: supermarket aisle with colorful product shelves
image_context: A supermarket aisle with colorful product shelves
example_1: Le rayon des vêtements est au deuxième étage.
example_1_translation: The clothing department is on the second floor.
example_2: Je cherche le rayon des produits frais.
example_2_translation: I'm looking for the fresh products aisle.
example_3: Il faut réapprovisionner le rayon des livres.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable. Masculine: le rayon
memory_hook: Rayon - from Latin 'radius' (ray/line) - lines of shelves
common_mistakes: It's masculine: le rayon, not la; pronounced ray-on

usage_notes: Feminine. Also means 'etiquette' in social context
common_collocations: étiquette prix, étiquette de vetement, lire l'étiquette
image_search_query: clothing price tag hanging from shirt
image_context: A clothing price tag hanging from a shirt
example_1: L'étiquette indique le prix.
example_2: Enlevez l'étiquette avant de porter.
example_2_translation: Remove the tag before wearing.
example_3: Selon l'étiquette, c'est 100% coton.
example_3_translation: According to the label, it's 100% cotton.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. Feminine: l'étiquette
memory_hook: Étiquette - from Old French 'estique' (small stick/label)
common_mistakes: It's feminine: l'étiquette, not le; double 't'

usage_notes: Masculine. Financial/commercial context
common_collocations: demander un remboursement, délai de remboursement, remboursement complet
image_search_query: money refund with receipt and cash on counter
image_context: Money refund with receipt and cash on counter
example_1: Je demande un remboursement.
example_2: Le remboursement peut prendre une semaine.
example_2_translation: The refund can take a week.
example_3: J'ai obtenu un remboursement complet.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. Masculine: le remboursement
memory_hook: Remboursement - re (again) + bourse (purse) - return money to purse
common_mistakes: It's masculine: le remboursement, not la; silent final 't'

usage_notes: Masculine. Plural same as singular: les taux
common_collocations: taux de change, taux de chômage, taux d'intérêt
image_search_query: financial charts showing percentage rates or statistics
image_context: Financial charts showing percentage rates rising
example_1: Le taux de change est avantageux.
example_2: Le taux de chômage augmente.
example_2_translation: The unemployment rate is increasing.
example_3: Quel est le taux d'intérêt?
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Masculine singular: le taux
memory_hook: Taux - from Latin 'taxus' (estimate/rate)
common_mistakes: Plural is same as singular: taux, not tauxes

usage_notes: Masculine plural. Singular: fichier
common_collocations: fichier informatique, gérer les fichiers, supprimer un fichier
image_search_query: computer folder icons representing file storage
image_context: Computer folder icons representing file storage
example_1: J'ai perdu mes fichiers importants.
example_2: Il faut trier les fichiers.
example_2_translation: The files need to be sorted.
example_3: Sauvegardez vos fichiers régulièrement.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable. Masculine plural: les fichiers
memory_hook: Fichier - from Latin 'ficta' (fixed/filing) - stored documents
common_mistakes: It's masculine plural: les fichiers, not la; singular fichier

usage_notes: Feminine. Computer/office equipment
common_collocations: imprimante laser, imprimante jet d'encre, papier imprimante
image_search_query: modern office printer printing document
image_context: A modern office printer printing a document
example_1: L'imprimante ne fonctionne pas.
example_2: J'ai besoin d'une nouvelle imprimante.
example_2_translation: I need a new printer.
example_3: Quelle sorte d'imprimante avez-vous?
example_3_translation: What kind of printer do you have?
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. Feminine: l'imprimante
memory_hook: Imprimante - imprimer (to print) + ante (agent) - printing agent
common_mistakes: It's feminine: l'imprimante, not le; pronounced ampre-mahnt

usage_notes: Masculine. French term for software
common_collocations: logiciel de traitement, installer un logiciel, logiciel libre
image_search_query: software installation screen on computer monitor
image_context: Software installation screen on a computer monitor
example_1: Je dois installer un nouveau logiciel.
example_1_translation: I need to install new software.
example_2: Le logiciel est trop lent.
example_2_translation: The software is too slow.
example_3: Quel logiciel utilisez-vous?
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. Masculine: le logiciel
memory_hook: Logiciel - logique (logic) + iel (tool) - logic tool
common_mistakes: It's masculine: le logiciel, not la; French term, not 'software'

usage_notes: Masculine. Usually uncountable
common_collocations: sur internet, connexion internet, page internet
image_search_query: global network visualization with connected web icons
image_context: Global network visualization with connected web icons
example_1: La connexion internet est lente.
example_2: Il passe beaucoup de temps sur internet.
example_2_translation: He spends a lot of time on the internet.
example_3: Avez-vous une connexion internet?
example_3_translation: Do you have an internet connection?
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Masculine: l'internet
memory_hook: Internet - inter (between) + net (network) - between networks
common_mistakes: Usually uncountable: internet, not 'internetS'; masculine

usage_notes: Masculine. Can mean TV program or software program
common_collocations: programme informatique, mettre à jour, programme TV
image_search_query: computer code editor with programming syntax
image_context: Computer code editor with programming syntax
example_1: J'ai lancé le programme.
example_2: Le programme doit être mis à jour.
example_2_translation: The program needs to be updated.
example_3: Quel est le programme de la journée?
example_3_translation: What's the schedule for the day?
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. Masculine: le programme
memory_hook: Programme - pro (before) + gramme (writing) - written before
related_words: logiciel, application, calendrier, emploi du temps
common_mistakes: It's masculine: le programme, not la; double 'm'

usage_notes: Feminine. Used for duration or periods
common_collocations: l'année dernière, l'année prochaine, toute l'année
image_search_query: calendar showing all twelve months of year
image_context: A calendar showing all twelve months of a year
example_1: J'ai habité ici pendant cinq années.
example_1_translation: I lived here for five years.
example_2: L'année prochaine, je voyage.
example_2_translation: Next year, I'm traveling.
example_3: Bonne année! (New Year greeting)
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable. Feminine: l'année
memory_hook: Année - from Latin 'annus' (year) - annual period
common_mistakes: It's feminine: l'année, not le; different from 'an'

usage_notes: Feminine. Starts Monday in French context
common_collocations: la semaine dernière, la semaine prochaine, toute la semaine
image_search_query: weekly planner calendar showing seven days
image_context: A weekly planner calendar showing seven days
example_1: Je pars en vacances la semaine prochaine.
example_1_translation: I'm leaving for vacation next week.
example_2: Cette semaine a été chargée.
example_2_translation: This week has been busy.
example_3: On se voit la semaine prochaine.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable. Feminine: la semaine
memory_hook: Semaine - from Latin 'septem' (seven) - seven day period
common_mistakes: It's feminine: la semaine, not le; starts with Monday in FR

secondary_meaning: occurring or done once a month
usage_notes: Adjective. Agrees with noun gender/number
common_collocations: magazine mensuel, paiement mensuel, rapport mensuel
image_search_query: monthly magazine issue on newsstand
image_context: A monthly magazine issue on a newsstand
example_1: Le magazine est mensuel.
example_2: Je fais un paiement mensuel.
example_2_translation: I make a monthly payment.
example_3: La réunion mensuelle est demain.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Feminine: mensuelle
memory_hook: Mensuel - mensis (Latin month) - related to month
common_mistakes: Feminine changes to mensuelle; don't confuse with 'menstruel'

usage_notes: Masculine. School/academic/business term
common_collocations: premier trimestre, deuxième trimestre, fin du trimestre
synonyms: quart d'année, période de trois mois
image_search_query: quarterly financial chart showing four periods
image_context: Quarterly financial chart showing four periods
example_1: Le premier trimestre est terminé.
example_2: Nous avons un examen au deuxième trimestre.
example_2_translation: We have an exam in the second trimester.
example_3: L'année scolaire est divisée en trois trimestres.
example_3_translation: The school year is divided into three terms.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Masculine: le trimestre
memory_hook: Trimestre - tri (three) + mestre (months) - three month period
common_mistakes: It's masculine: le trimestre, not la; three months, not four

usage_notes: Feminine. Often used for historical periods
common_collocations: la décennie suivante, à la fin de la décennie, les années 2020
synonyms: période de dix ans, dix années
image_search_query: timeline showing ten years marked as decades
image_context: A timeline showing ten years marked as decades
example_1: Cette décennie a vu beaucoup de changements.
example_1_translation: This decade has seen many changes.
example_2: Dans une décennie, tout sera différent.
example_2_translation: In a decade, everything will be different.
example_3: La décennie des années 80 était intéressante.
example_3_translation: The decade of the 1980s was interesting.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable. Feminine: la décennie
memory_hook: Décennie - déca (ten) - ten year period
common_mistakes: It's feminine: la décennie, not le; pronounced dek-enn-ee

usage_notes: Feminine. Informal short form of météorologie
common_collocations: regarder la météo, selon la météo, météo de demain
image_search_query: weather forecast map showing sun and clouds
image_context: Weather forecast map showing sun and clouds icons
example_1: Quelle est la météo aujourd'hui?
example_2: La météo annonce de la pluie.
example_2_translation: The forecast announces rain.
example_3: Regardons la météo avant de partir.
example_3_translation: Let's check the weather before leaving.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Feminine: la météo
memory_hook: Météo - short for météorologie (meteorology)
common_mistakes: It's feminine: la météo, not le; informal short form

usage_notes: Feminine. Can be literal or figurative (upheaval)
common_collocations: tempête de neige, tempête de sable, une terrible tempête
image_search_query: violent thunderstorm with lightning over dark sea
image_context: A violent thunderstorm with lightning over a dark sea
example_1: Une tempête approche.
example_2: La tempête a causé beaucoup de dégâts.
example_2_translation: The storm caused a lot of damage.
example_3: Il y a une tempête dehors.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Feminine: la tempête
memory_hook: Tempête - from Latin 'tempestas' (weather/season) - bad weather
common_mistakes: It's feminine: la tempête, not le; has circumflex accent

usage_notes: Feminine. Usually uncountable
common_collocations: orage de grêle, une grêle de, grêlons
image_search_query: ice hailstones scattered on ground during hailstorm
image_context: Ice hailstones scattered on ground during a hailstorm
example_1: Il y a de la grêle ce matin.
example_2: La grêle a endommagé les voitures.
example_2_translation: Hail damaged the cars.
example_3: La grêle s'abattait sur le toit.
example_3_translation: Hail was hammering on the roof.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Feminine: la grêle
memory_hook: Grêle - from Latin 'grandinem' (hail) - ice balls
common_mistakes: It's feminine: la grêle, not le; usually uncountable

usage_notes: Adjective. Invariable for masculine/feminine
common_collocations: climat humide, air humide, peau humide
image_search_query: drops of water on humid window pane
image_context: Drops of water on a humid window pane
example_1: Le climat est très humide ici.
example_1_translation: The climate is very humid here.
example_2: Il fait humide aujourd'hui.
example_2_translation: It's humid today.
example_3: Une journée humide et chaude.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable. Pronounced yoo-mid
memory_hook: Humide - from Latin 'humidus' (damp) - containing moisture
common_mistakes: Doesn't change for gender/number; pronounced 'umid' not 'humide'

usage_notes: Adjective. Feminine: ensoleillée
common_collocations: jour ensoleillé, très ensoleillé, région ensoleillée
image_search_query: bright sunny beach with clear blue sky and sun
image_context: Bright sunny beach with clear blue sky and sun
example_1: La journée est ensoleillée.
example_2: Nous avons un temps ensoleillé.
example_2_translation: We have sunny weather.
example_3: C'est une région très ensoleillée.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. Feminine: ensoleillée
memory_hook: Ensoleillé - en (in) + soleil (sun) + é (past part.) - filled with sun
common_mistakes: Feminine changes to ensoleillée; means 'sunny' not 'sunny-colored'

secondary_meaning: the art of painting or a painted artwork
usage_notes: Feminine. Can mean paint (substance) or artwork
common_collocations: faire de la peinture, peinture à l'huile, tableau de peinture
image_search_query: artist painting colorful landscape on canvas
image_context: An artist painting a colorful landscape on canvas
example_1: La peinture est ma passion.
example_2: Il fait de la peinture le week-end.
example_2_translation: He paints on weekends.
example_3: Cette peinture est très belle.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable. Feminine: la peinture
memory_hook: Peinture - peindre (to paint) + ure (noun suffix)
common_mistakes: It's feminine: la peinture, not le; means both paint and artwork

secondary_meaning: the art or practice of taking photographs
usage_notes: Feminine. Also means a photograph (single image)
common_collocations: faire de la photographie, photographie numérique, appareil photographie
image_search_query: photographer holding professional camera outdoors
image_context: A photographer holding a professional camera outdoors
example_1: La photographie m'intéresse beaucoup.
example_2: Il a un appareil de photographie professionnel.
example_2_translation: He has a professional camera.
example_3: J'aime faire de la photographie.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. Feminine: la photographie
memory_hook: Photographie - photo (light) + graphie (writing) - writing with light
common_mistakes: It's feminine: la photographie, not le; pronounced fo-to-graf-fee

usage_notes: Feminine. Often for mountain or countryside walks
common_collocations: faire une randonnée, randonnée pédestre, randonnée guidée
image_search_query: hikers walking along mountain trail at sunrise
image_context: Hikers walking along a mountain trail at sunrise
example_1: Nous avons fait une randonnée en montagne.
example_1_translation: We went hiking in the mountains.
example_2: La randonnée dure environ trois heures.
example_2_translation: The hike lasts about three hours.
example_3: J'aime faire des randonnées le week-end.
example_3_translation: I like going hiking on weekends.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. Feminine: la randonnée
memory_hook: Randonnée - from randan (random) - random walking journey
common_mistakes: It's feminine: la randonnée, not le; pronounced ran-don-ay

secondary_meaning: activity of staying in tents; camping site
usage_notes: Masculine. English loanword in French
common_collocations: faire du camping, terrain de camping, tente de camping
image_search_query: tent camping site by lake under starry sky
image_context: A tent camping site by a lake under starry sky
example_1: Nous allons faire du camping cet été.
example_2: Le camping est près du lac.
example_2_translation: The camping site is near the lake.
example_3: Le camping en montagne est agréable.
example_3_translation: Camping in the mountains is pleasant.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable. Masculine: le camping
memory_hook: Camping - English word adopted in French - outdoor camping
common_mistakes: It's masculine: le camping, not la; English loanword

usage_notes: Feminine. Often: plongée sous-marine (scuba diving)
common_collocations: plongée sous-marine, faire de la plongée, club de plongée
image_search_query: scuba diver exploring colorful coral reef underwater
image_context: A scuba diver exploring colorful coral reef underwater
example_1: La plongée sous-marine est spectaculaire.
example_2: Je fais de la plongée depuis cinq ans.
example_2_translation: I've been diving for five years.
example_3: La plongée requiert une formation.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable. Feminine: la plongée
memory_hook: Plongée - plonger (to dive) + ée (feminine) - diving action
common_mistakes: It's feminine: la plongée, not le; pronounced plon-zhay

secondary_meaning: art or practice of sailing or navigating
usage_notes: Feminine. Also means web navigation
common_collocations: navigation de plaisance, faire de la navigation, club de navigation
image_search_query: sailboat navigating through calm blue waters
image_context: A sailboat navigating through calm blue waters
example_1: La navigation sur ce fleuve est dangereuse.
example_1_translation: Navigation on this river is dangerous.
example_2: Il pratique la navigation de plaisance.
example_2_translation: He practices recreational sailing.
example_3: Je fais de la navigation le week-end.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. Feminine: la navigation
memory_hook: Navigation - naviguer (to sail) + tion (noun) - sailing action
common_mistakes: It's feminine: la navigation, not le; pronounced nav-ee-gah-see-on

secondary_meaning: a group of items gathered together
usage_notes: Feminine. Often for hobbies and gatherings
common_collocations: collection de timbres, collection de poupées, ajouter à la collection
image_search_query: collection of vintage stamps arranged in album
image_context: A collection of vintage stamps arranged in album
example_1: Il a une collection de timbres.
example_2: La collection d'art est impressionnante.
example_2_translation: The art collection is impressive.
example_3: Sa collection de disques est remarquable.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable. Feminine: la collection
memory_hook: Collection - collectionner (to collect) + tion - gathered items
common_mistakes: It's feminine: la collection, not le; pronounced kol-lek-see-on
